boundaries: Keep planning focused on VM-22 only.
Keep VM-20 out of scope during this wave.
Keep VM-21 out of scope during this wave.
Keep VM-25 and later chapters out of scope; page 318 is only the closing boundary before VM-25 begins on page 319.
Keep VM-30, VM-31, VM-G, VM-C, and VM-M references review-only unless the same batch intentionally includes the referenced operational text.
Keep stale, superseded, repealed, companion, or implementation guidance as notes only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No VM-25 or later chapter processing except the closing boundary confirmation on page 318.

topicMap.label: Background, risks, and applicability
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the background, risk categories, and scope / effective-date language review-only and stop before Section 3 reserve methodology.

topicMap.label: Reserve methodology and aggregate reserve structure
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the aggregate reserve structure, the reinsurance basis, and the three-month timing limit separate from the Section 4 projection mechanics.

topicMap.label: Section 4 projection entry
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the projected accumulated-deficiency setup and cash-flow directions together, but stop before the later asset mechanics.

topicMap.label: Section 4 asset projection and NAER
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the additional invested asset portfolio, NAER, borrowing, and annuitization context separate from the reinsurance section.

topicMap.label: Reinsurance
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep pre- and post-reinsurance reserve treatment separate from the Section 6 opener.

topicMap.label: Standard projection amount overview
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the CTEPA overview separate from the detailed contract mechanics.

topicMap.label: Standard projection contract mechanics
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep GAPV, partial withdrawals, and the contract-type mechanics review-only.

topicMap.label: Standard projection lapse and rate factors
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep lapse, MVA, GMIR, and rate-factor language separate from the mortality tables.

topicMap.label: Standard projection mortality tables I
topicMap.boundaryNote: Capture the first mortality-table block and the structured-settlement formula context.

topicMap.label: Standard projection mortality tables II and closing boundary
topicMap.boundaryNote: Finish the Section 6 tables, group-annuity guidance, and the elective provisions up to the Section 7 boundary.

topicMap.label: Stochastic exclusion and single scenario testing
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the stochastic exclusion rules separate from the scenario-generation section.

topicMap.label: Scenario generation
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the scenario-generation rules separate from the hedging strategy section.

topicMap.label: Hedging under a future non-index credit hedging strategy
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the hedging strategy, error-factor treatment, and documentation expectations review-only.

topicMap.label: Contract holder behavior assumptions
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep behavior assumptions and eligibility / termination logic distinct from the mortality section.

topicMap.label: Mortality assumptions
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep mortality credibility and adjustment language separate from the remaining assumption guidance.

topicMap.label: Other assumptions
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the margin, expense, and general assumption guidance review-only.

topicMap.label: Allocation of aggregate reserves and closing boundary
topicMap.boundaryNote: Close the chapter cleanly and stop before VM-25 begins on page 319.

proposedBatches.title: VM-22 background, risks, and scope

proposedBatches.rationale: Anchor the chapter frame and applicability before reserve methodology begins.

pageTarget.notes: Capture the aggregate reserve structure, the reinsurance basis, and the three-month timing limit.

pageTarget.notes: Capture the CTEPA overview, aggregation effects, and the DR / SR reduction framing.

pageTarget.notes: Capture GAPV, partial withdrawals, and the contract-type mechanics used by the additional projection amount.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep the Section 6 contract mechanics narrow and review-only.

pageTarget.notes: Capture base lapse, MVA / GMIR factors, and rate-factor tables.

proposedBatches.rationale: Separate lapse and rate-factor rules from the mortality table blocks.

pageTarget.notes: Capture the first mortality-table block and the structured-settlement formula context.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep the first mortality-table set narrow before the remaining table-heavy pages.

proposedBatches.title: VM-22 standard projection mortality tables II and closing boundary

pageTarget.notes: Finish the Section 6 tables, group-annuity guidance, and the elective provisions up to the Section 7 boundary.

proposedBatches.rationale: Finish Section 6 cleanly and stop before stochastic exclusion tests begin.

pageTarget.notes: Capture stochastic exclusion and single scenario testing.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep the stochastic exclusion rules separate from scenario generation.

proposedBatches.rationale: Separate scenario-generation rules from the hedging strategy section.

pageTarget.notes: Capture the hedging strategy framework, error-factor treatment, and documentation expectations.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep hedging and risk-mitigation material review-only and distinct from behavior assumptions.

pageTarget.notes: Capture lapse, withdrawal, transfer, deposit, and termination behavior assumptions.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep behavior assumptions separate from mortality and the later assumption guidance.

pageTarget.notes: Capture prudent-estimate mortality guidance and credibility treatment.

pageTarget.notes: Capture contract-level allocation and stop before VM-25 begins on page 319.

proposedBatches.rationale: Close the chapter cleanly and keep VM-25 and later chapters out of the wave.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat explicit must, shall, or required language as the requirement; preserve the citation and chapter context.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat terms, chapter navigation, and term-boundary clarifications as terminology content unless they impose a duty.
reviewStandards.reserveMethodStructure: Treat reserve-method organization, aggregation, and reserve-floor language as structural method content.
reviewStandards.calculationStructure: Treat formulas, formula steps, and calculation ordering as calculation structure.
reviewStandards.formulaContext: Keep formulas with their setup terms, defined variables, and immediate explanatory context.
reviewStandards.prescribedAssumption: Treat prescribed assumption values or formula inputs as fixed-source assumptions.
reviewStandards.companyOrPrudentEstimateAssumption: Treat prudent-estimate or company-selected assumptions as review-only until the actuarial basis is clear.
reviewStandards.scenarioOrStochasticRequirement: Treat scenario generation, stochastic projections, and simulation rules as stochastic requirements.
reviewStandards.assetModelingJudgment: Treat asset mix, investment strategy, hedge selection, and proxy mapping as human judgment unless the text is explicit.
reviewStandards.hedgingOrRiskMitigation: Treat hedging, derivative, and risk-mitigation language as review-only unless the section fully spells out the rule.
reviewStandards.reinsurance: Treat ceded and assumed reinsurance cash flows, treaty features, and reserve adjustments as review-only until the operational treatment is fully captured.
reviewStandards.reportingRequirement: Treat filing, submission, report, and disclosure requirements as reporting requirements.
reviewStandards.documentationExpectation: Treat retention, memorandum, support-file, and evidence language as documentation expectations.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to other VM sections, models, or appendices as mapping notes unless the operational text is included.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat introductions, examples, navigation text, and chapter background as background content unless it imposes a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat boundary slices and transition pages as review-only windows that should not be merged forward.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat assumptions, margins, reserve interpretations, and boundary cases as requiring human actuarial interpretation unless the text is explicit.
reviewStandards.staleSupersededCompanionGuidance: Treat stale, superseded, companion, or implementation guidance as review-only support and do not promote it.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The VM-22 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch is review-only by default.
The plan explicitly excludes VM-20, VM-21, and VM-25 or later chapters except for the closing boundary confirmation.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The VM-22 batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: Chapter-window checks for the confirmed VM-22 page range 227-318.
Cross-reference flag coverage for VM-20, VM-21, VM-30, VM-31, VM-G, VM-C, and VM-M.
No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
Batch-definition synchronization once VM-22 extraction starts.
scriptImplications: scripts/vm22-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-038 onward sequence when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the VM-22 definition file so the shared runner can find the planned batch IDs.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the VM-22 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the closing boundary before VM-25.
No dedicated npm shortcut is required because the shared runner already accepts --batch for each VM-22 batch.

notes: Controlled VM-22 planning artifact. Planned batches 038-054 remain review-only by default and are not extraction runs.
